pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicit text addressing Lab Teaching Assistant from Prof. Reynolds
setup_task.sh instructions and NOAA data format
export_result.sh checks for these specific files

Python analysis of real NOAA long-term mean SST data extracting values for February (Time Index 1). Measured values directly from dataset: SUB_WEST (~22.6C), SUB_EAST (~18.0C), MID_WEST (~0.1C), MID_EAST (~12.5C). All bounds validated.
Extracted numerical values logically dictate WEST is warmer at 30N (22.6C vs 18.0C) and EAST is warmer at 45N (12.5C vs 0.1C).

privileged_info_summary: The task utilizes a real-world NOAA OI SST v2 long-term mean dataset (sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc), securely downloaded from official NOAA FTP servers. Direct numerical data extraction using Python confirms the ground truth sea surface temperatures in February (time index 1) at the exact target coordinates: ~22.6°C for SUB_WEST (30N, 78W), ~18.0°C for SUB_EAST (30N, 12W), ~0.1°C for MID_WEST (45N, 60W), and ~12.5°C for MID_EAST (45N, 12W). These empirical values perfectly validate the task's expected numerical boundaries and explicitly confirm the requested expected logic: the western boundary current is warmer in the subtropics (30°N), while the eastern boundary current is warmer in the mid-latitudes (45°N).
